articles.title: Password Reset Guide
articles.content:
If you've forgotten your password, follow these steps to reset it:

1. Navigate to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'
2. Enter your registered email address
3. Check your inbox for the reset link (check spam/junk folders)
4. Click the link within 24 hours (it expires after that)
5. Enter your new password (minimum 8 characters, must include uppercase, lowercase, and a number)
6. Log in with your new password

If you don't receive the reset email within 5 minutes:
- Check your spam/junk folder
- Verify you're using the correct email address
- Contact support for manual password reset

For security, password reset links can only be used once and expire after 24 hours.
articles.tags: password, reset, email, recovery, forgot, login

articles.title: SSO Configuration Guide
articles.content:
Setting up Single Sign-On (SSO) for your organization:

Supported Providers:
- Okta (SAML 2.0)
- Google Workspace (OIDC)
- Azure Active Directory (coming soon)

Setup Steps:
1. Navigate to Settings → Authentication → SSO
2. Select your identity provider
3. Enter your SAML endpoint URL or OIDC client credentials
4. Upload your IdP metadata XML file
5. Configure attribute mappings (email, name, department)
6. Test the connection with a pilot group
7. Enable SSO for all users

Important Notes:
- SSO is available on Enterprise plans only
- Users will be auto-provisioned on first login
- Existing accounts will be linked by email address
- Admin accounts can always use password fallback
articles.tags: sso, saml, azure, okta, google, single sign-on, authentication

articles.title: Dashboard Performance Tuning
articles.content:
If your dashboard is loading slowly, try these optimization steps:

Quick Fixes:
1. Reduce the date range filter (default is 90 days, try 30 days)
2. Disable real-time updates in Settings → Dashboard → Auto-refresh
3. Remove unused widgets from your dashboard layout
4. Clear your browser cache and cookies

Advanced Optimization:
1. Enable data caching: Settings → Performance → Enable Cache
2. Set cache TTL to 5 minutes for non-critical widgets
3. Use the 'Lite' dashboard view for large datasets (>10,000 records)
4. Schedule heavy reports for off-peak hours

For Administrators:
- Check database query performance in Admin → Diagnostics
- Add composite indexes for frequently filtered columns
- Consider archiving data older than 1 year
- Monitor connection pool usage in the health dashboard
articles.tags: dashboard, slow, loading, cache, performance, optimization, speed

articles.title: API Rate Limiting Configuration
articles.content:
Understanding and configuring API rate limits:

Default Limits:
- Free tier: 60 requests/minute
- Pro tier: 300 requests/minute
- Enterprise tier: 1,000 requests/minute

Rate Limit Headers:
All API responses include these headers:
- X-RateLimit-Limit: Maximum requests per window
- X-RateLimit-Remaining: Requests remaining in current window
- X-RateLimit-Reset: Unix timestamp when the window resets

Handling Rate Limits:
When you exceed the limit, you'll receive a 429 Too Many Requests response. Best practices:
1. Implement exponential backoff (start with 1 second, double each retry)
2. Cache responses when possible
3. Use bulk endpoints instead of individual calls
4. Monitor your usage via the API dashboard

Custom Rate Limits:
Enterprise customers can request custom rate limits by contacting support.

articles.title: Monthly Report Export Guide
articles.content:
How to export monthly usage and analytics reports:

Export Steps:
1. Navigate to Settings → Reports
2. Select the report type (Usage, Analytics, Billing, or Custom)
3. Choose the date range (select the month you need)
4. Select the export format:
   - CSV: Best for data analysis in Excel or Google Sheets
   - PDF: Best for sharing with stakeholders
   - JSON: Best for programmatic processing
5. Click 'Generate Report'
6. Download will start automatically (large reports may take a few minutes)

Scheduled Reports:
You can set up automatic monthly reports:
1. Go to Settings → Reports → Scheduled
2. Click 'New Schedule'
3. Select report type and format
4. Choose delivery method (email or webhook)
5. Set the schedule (e.g., 1st of every month)

Note: Reports include data up to the previous day. Real-time data is available in the dashboard.
articles.tags: export, csv, pdf, monthly, reports, analytics, download

articles.title: Server Error (5xx) Troubleshooting
articles.content:
Troubleshooting guide for server errors (500, 502, 503, 504):

500 Internal Server Error:
- Usually caused by application-level issues
- Check recent deployments for breaking changes
- Review application logs for stack traces
- Verify database connectivity and connection pool status
- Check disk space and memory usage

502 Bad Gateway:
- The upstream server returned an invalid response
- Check if the application process is running
- Verify reverse proxy configuration (nginx/Apache)
- Check for network issues between proxy and app server

503 Service Unavailable:
- Server is overloaded or under maintenance
- Check CPU and memory usage
- Review auto-scaling configuration
- Wait for maintenance window to complete

504 Gateway Timeout:
- Upstream server took too long to respond
- Check for slow database queries
- Review timeout settings in proxy config
- Consider increasing timeout for long-running operations

General Steps:
1. Check the status page: status.example.com
2. Review application logs in Admin → Logs
3. Check recent deployments in Admin → Deployments
4. Contact support if the issue persists beyond 30 minutes

articles.title: Billing and Payment FAQ
articles.content:
Frequently asked questions about billing and payments:

Q: What payment methods do you accept?
A: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and PayPal. Enterprise customers can pay by invoice (NET 30).

Q: How do I update my payment method?
A: Go to Settings → Billing → Payment Methods → Add/Edit.

Q: Can I switch plans mid-cycle?
A: Yes! Upgrades are prorated and take effect immediately. Downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle.

Q: Do you offer annual billing discounts?
A: Yes, annual billing saves 20% compared to monthly billing. Contact sales for enterprise pricing.

Q: How do I cancel my subscription?
A: Go to Settings → Billing → Cancel Subscription. Your data will be retained for 30 days after cancellation.

Q: Where can I find my invoices?
A: Settings → Billing → Invoice History. Invoices are also emailed to the billing contact on file.

Q: Do you offer refunds?
A: We offer a 14-day money-back guarantee for new subscriptions. Contact support for refund requests.
articles.tags: billing, payment, invoice, subscription, pricing, refund, cancel

articles.title: User Role and Permission Setup
articles.content:
Managing user roles and permissions in your organization:

Available Roles:
1. Owner: Full access, billing management, can delete organization
2. Admin: User management, settings, all features
3. Manager: Team management, report access, feature configuration
4. Member: Standard access to assigned features and data
5. Viewer: Read-only access to dashboards and reports

Setting Up Roles:
1. Navigate to Settings → Team → Members
2. Click on a user's name to edit their role
3. Select the new role from the dropdown
4. Click 'Save Changes'

Custom Roles (Enterprise):
Enterprise customers can create custom roles:
1. Go to Settings → Team → Roles
2. Click 'Create Custom Role'
3. Name the role and select permissions
4. Assign the role to team members

Permission Categories:
- Data Access: View, create, edit, delete records
- Admin: User management, billing, settings
- Reports: View, export, schedule reports
- API: API key management, webhook configuration

Best Practices:
- Follow the principle of least privilege
- Review permissions quarterly
- Use custom roles for specialized access needs
articles.tags: roles, permissions, admin, access, users, team, security
